entry.title: ITSMobile - Zebra - Velocity browser
entry.content:
We are running some old RF devices on SAPConsole with Telnet and are using the solution from OSS note 371838 (NOTIFY_BELL_SIGNAL) to get a sound from the device.

We have also some Zebra MC330K running from SAP ITS. On this Android device we are running on the Velocity browser. IT has chosen this to lock down for unwanted features in the browser. But the result is that the old solution with sound don't work anymore.

Are somebody running this combination ?

The browser basically just supports the IDA Command IDA_BEEP and IDA_ERROR_BEEP

The only way to send this sound is by using code from this : https://help.ivanti.com/wl/help/de_DE/Velocity/1.1/Velocity/DevelopingPages/IDA_Commands.htm

Basically I have modified the SOUND.HTML that was added with ~ITSMOBILESOUNDINCLUDE

 

The sound.html looks like this 

<script type="text/javascript" language="javascript">
`if ( ( ~messageline != "" ) && ( ~itsmobilemsgsound == "1" ) || ( ~currdynpro.messagesound != "" ) )
if ( ~currdynpro.messagesound == "S" )
<!-- sucess message -->
` location.href = "ida:IDA_BEEP" ;
` elseif ( ~currdynpro.messagesound == "I" )
<!-- information message -->
` location.href = "ida:IDA_BEEP" ;
` elseif ( ~currdynpro.messagesound == "W" )
<!-- warning message -->
` location.href = "ida:IDA_ERROR_BEEP" ;
` elseif ( ~currdynpro.messagesound == "E" )
<!-- error message -->
` location.href = "ida:IDA_ERROR_BEEP" ;
` else
<!-- message type not set -->
` location.href = "ida:IDA_ERROR_BEEP" ;
` end;
end;`
</script>

I can't get "document.location" or "window.navigate" to give a sound. And the problem with "location.href" is that it displays a empty screen, when I have to use Back un the device to get back to the screen.

 

Has anyone a solution for this combination ?

entry.title: Register Now for Our Exclusive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!
entry.content:
GROW with SAP is a comprehensive package combining solutions, services, community support, and learnings to help businesses of all sizes successfully adopt cloud ERP. At its core lies SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, our fully-fledged cloud ERP designed to optimize processes and drive intelligent, sustainable operations. 

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Early Release Series is a biannual event showcasing live sessions led by product experts. These sessions provide an exclusive preview of upcoming innovations, including AI-driven features and advancements with Joule, tailored to selected lines of business and industries. Spanning several days, the series offers expert demos, insider previews, and real-time Q&A, giving participants a first look at exciting updates and the opportunity to get their questions answered directly.

From January 21 to January 24, 2025, we'll start the year with 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you.

Agenda:

 

👉Don't miss this opportunity and register now! 

 

Sign-Up Instructions:

An active SAP Learning Hub license is required to register for the live sessions and to consume the recordings. Find out how to sign up​

  • As a customer:​ Access to at least the SAP Enterprise Support Edition is required. Follow these steps to sign up:​
    1. Navigate to the sign-up page and click on the “sign up” button.​
    2. Authenticate yourself with your S-user ID and password.​
    3. The system will check your eligibility, create a new SAP Learning Hub user, and populate your learning catalogue. This process may take up to 2 hours.​
    4. You will receive a registration confirmation via e-mail once the registration process is complete.​
  • As a partner: Partners can access the live sessions and recordings through the SAP Partner Edge Edition.​
    ​
  • As an SAP internal: Register here for the SAP Learning Hub Internal Edition (free of charge) by clicking on "access".​

Experiencing issues? Make sure to be logged on before registering. In case of any other issues, reach out to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Live Product Expert Sessions.

Find out more about our live product expert sessions here.

entry.title: How to Handle Empty Data Arrays in MDK App to Prevent Crashes?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm currently working on an MDK app and have encountered an issue where the app crashes when the data array is empty. Specifically, I've set clientData as the target for a data table, but when clientData is an empty array ([]), the app crashes upon opening the page. In previous MDK V24.7.5 if there is not data then data is not visible, but in mdk 24.11 if data table target value is empty then it is crashing.

Here are the steps I've taken so far:

  1. Ensured that the MDK app is updated to the latest version.
  2. Tried to implement data validation to check if the array is empty before binding it to the table.
  3. Added error handling to catch any exceptions during the data binding process.

Despite these efforts, the app still crashes when clientData is empty. Has anyone else faced a similar issue? How did you resolve it? Any suggestions or best practices would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - Discrete Orders - Sampling Requirements (dif...
entry.content: Hi @ppilvines1, this is not enough input to clearly understand your production process/flow, requirements, constraints and the level of shop floor automation and integration that you have available, if any. I can probably think of several ways to implement a sampling logic, from really simple to really complex, once again depending on your specific requirements. In general, you should be able to model data collections to reflect the different steps required and use Automatic Triggers to catch manual by workers and/or automatic by machines production events and trigger a PPD to check actual quantity versus sample rate for that specific manufacturing context, to either collect data automatically if possible, trigger an alert so workers can do it manually, or do something to block the SFC like place a hold, disposition or perhaps start a special operation to enforce the data collection before the SFC can proceed, if it´s not a continuous process, for example. Hope it helps somehow. Br, Manoel

entry.title: Re: Can user has user defined header/item condition in Sa...
entry.content:
Hello @Ravi_Mehta 

This is the standard behavior of the system: within a single pricing procedure, you cannot activate multiple base price conditions simultaneously. For instance, if the system determines the PR00 condition type based on an existing condition record, and the user enters another base price condition in the sales order, the system will automatically deactivate the PR00 condition type and activate the new base price condition

Best Regards

Vivek Savant.

entry.title: Re: RE: CAP Java Documentation -- Copy & Modify CDS QL St...
entry.content:
++ Replace the where clause with another where condition:

CqnSelect copy = CQL.copy(query, new Modifier() {
   @Override
   public Predicate where(Predicate where) {
      return CQL.get("<column-name-from-entity>").in(["<val1>", "<val2>"]);
   }
})

 Filter out specific segments:

CqnSelect copy = CQL.copy(query, new Modifier() {
    @Override
    public Predicate comparison(Value<?> lhs, Operator op, Value<?> rhs) {
        if (lhs.isRef() && lhs.asRef().lastSegment().equals("<column-name>") {
            return CQL.constant(true).eq(CQL.constant(true));
        }
        return CQL.comparison(lhs, op, rhs);
    }
})

entry.title: Re: Can user has user defined header/item condition in Sa...
entry.content:
Hello @Ravi_Mehta 

In the Control condition type control, there is a field called "Condition Class," which determines the nature of a pricing condition. The classifications are as follows:

  • B: Base Price
  • A: Discount
  • D: Taxes

and So On

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract for Sales - Manual Delta Accruals
entry.content:
Dear,

Booking invoices appear to be a workaround to handle a common use case that is not covered by standard functionality. This issue is particularly critical in S/4HANA brownfield transformation projects, where closing rebates using the "old" functionality is not always feasible

Additionally, this approach may only be viable during the data migration phase, where we could use "fake" billing documents not posted to accounting. Otherwise, the turnover could be overstated. (Note: Ideally, we should be able to define business volume by selecting billing documents not posted to accounting. However, this introduces added complexity to the business volume selection criteria defined in the condition contract.)

This workaround cannot be proposed for day-to-day operations, as it would involve creating fake billing documents or updating the condition contract (CC) for REJ1 each time users post an unplanned manual delta accrual.

In conclusion, I believe we need a customized solution to effectively manage this use case.

Thank you

Michele

entry.title: Re: CL_BCS Creates folder in client's outlook for PDF print.
entry.content:
This issue got solved,

The actual issue is with acknowledge mails like “Your message has been delivered to the following recipients:” or “Not delivered” which will create unnecessary folder in Outlook I have used below values to suppress these Acknowledge mails/ Folders.

For CL_BCS class

lo_bcs->SET_STATUS_ATTRIBUTES( I_REQUESTED_STATUS = 'N'  ).   

For SO_NEW_DOCUMENT_ATT_SEND_API1 FM

  RECEIVERS-NOTIF_DEL  = ' '.
  RECEIVERS-NOTIF_NDEL = ' '.

entry.title: CDS : How to define analytical query using two structures
entry.content:
This blog is about how to use two structures in CDS analytical query as in BW query.

The structure forms the foundational layout of the axes (rows or columns) in a data table, determining how information is organized and presented.
Structural components of key figure structures are always based on the key figure selections (basic key figures, restricted key figures, and calculated key figures). Characteristic structural components cannot contain key figure selections.

The arrangement of the structure affects the order and quantity of key figures or characteristics displayed in the rows and columns of a query or report.

Example: BW Query with Characteristic structure and Key Figure structure

In Analytical query, elements can be assigned to characteristics structure using @AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true. Element with measures get automatically assigned to measure structure.

Formulas can be defined for both characteristic structure and measure structure. The operands of a formula must be elements of the same structure to which the formula belongs. Operands of the other structure are not allowed.

If an analytical query has two structures (a measure and a characteristic structure), then the query result is a grid. Each cell in this grid is defined by an element of the measure structure and one of the characteristic structures. Restrictions are combined with AND. If both elements are formulas, the formula of the measure structure is calculated by default. Also, other properties like scaling, decimals are taken from the element of the measure structure by default. If this should be different, @AnalyticsDetails.query.collisionHandling annotation is used.

  collisionHandling:

      { formula        : String( 20) enum { DEFAULT; THIS; CONCURRENT; };

        decimals       : String( 20) enum { DEFAULT; THIS; CONCURRENT; };

        scaling        : String( 20) enum { DEFAULT; THIS; CONCURRENT; };

        semanticObject : String( 20) enum { DEFAULT; THIS; CONCURRENT; };

      };

Example:

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS Query with 2 Structures'
@ObjectModel.modelingPattern: #ANALYTICAL_QUERY
@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [ #ANALYTICAL_QUERY ]

define transient view entity ZLR_2STRUCT_CELL_REF_SIMPLE
provider contract analytical_query
as projection on ZOQ_FLIGHT
{

//----Elements of Key Figure structure
@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@EndUserText.label: 'Max Capacity Economy'
seatsmax,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@EndUserText.label: 'Occupied Economy'
seatsocc,


@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@Aggregation.default: #FORMULA
@EndUserText.label: 'Percentage'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.decimals: 5
seatsocc / seatsmax * 100 as percentage,


//---Elements of Characteristics Structure

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
@EndUserText.label: 'Calender Year 2019'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true
case when flyear = '2019' then 1 else null end as year2019,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
@EndUserText.label: 'Calender Year 2020'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true
case when flyear = '2020' then 1 else null end as year2020,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
@Aggregation.default: #FORMULA
@EndUserText.label: 'Diffenece 2019-2020'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true
@AnalyticsDetails.query.decimals: 2
@AnalyticsDetails.query.collisionHandling: {decimals: #THIS}
 $projection.year2019 -  $projection.year2020 as difference

}

 

Output: 

To display structure element in a hierarchy, AnalyticsDetials.query.elementHierarchy.parent and @AnalyticsDetails.query.elementHierarchy.initiallyCollapsed annotations are used in CDS.

Example:

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS Query with 2 Structures'
@ObjectModel.modelingPattern: #ANALYTICAL_QUERY
@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [ #ANALYTICAL_QUERY ]

define transient view entity ZLR_2STRUCT_CELL_REF_HIER
provider contract analytical_query
as projection on ZOQ_FLIGHT
{

//----Elements of Key Figure structure
@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@EndUserText.label: 'Max Capacity Economy'
seatsmax,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@EndUserText.label: 'Occupied Economy'
seatsocc,


@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@Aggregation.default: #FORMULA
@EndUserText.label: 'Percentage'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.decimals: 5
seatsocc / seatsmax * 100 as percentage,


//---Elements of Characteristics Structure

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
@EndUserText.label: 'Calender Year 2019'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.elementHierarchy.parent: 'difference'
case when flyear = '2019' then 1 else null end as year2019,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
@EndUserText.label: 'Calender Year 2020'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.elementHierarchy.parent: 'difference'
case when flyear = '2020' then 1 else null end as year2020,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
@Aggregation.default: #FORMULA
@EndUserText.label: 'Diffenece 2019-2020'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true
@AnalyticsDetails.query.decimals: 2
@AnalyticsDetails.query.collisionHandling: {decimals: #THIS}
@AnalyticsDetails.query.elementHierarchy.initiallyCollapsed: true  
$projection.year2019 -  $projection.year2020 as difference

}

 

Output: 

 

After Expanding Hierarchy

Reference:

Working with Structures

entry.title: Re: Adobe form - Formcalc/Javascript How can we get the l...
entry.content:
For the benefit of all here is the solution applied.

1. We need a flowed empty subform between the "frm_Signature" and the previous subform

  • subform "frm_Detail"
  • subform "frm_Filler"
  • subform "frm_Signature"

2.  At the event "ready:layout" of the "frm_Signature" subform add the following Javascript

var subform = "frm_Signature" ;
var position = 190 ; // Desire bottom position in mm 
var page = xfa.layout.absPageCount() - 1 ;
var objs = xfa.layout.pageContent(page, "subform") ;
for (var i=0; i<objs.length; i++) {
   var som = objs.item(i).somExpression ;
   if ( som.indexOf(subform) != -1 ){
      var object = objs.item(i);
      break;
   }
}
var objectH = xfa.layout.h( object, "mm" );   
var objectY = xfa.layout.y( object, "mm" );
if ( ( objectY + objectH ) < position ) {
   this.parent.frm_Filler.h = position - ( objectY + objectH ) + "mm";  // Assign height of frm_Filler
   xfa.layout.relayout();
}

This will assign the height of the "frm_Filler" subform and relaunch the layout process.

entry.title: Missing Data source FDP_EA_REQST_FOR_QUOT_INT_SRV in SEGW
entry.content:
Hello all

We need to enhance the RFQ form "MM_PUR_RFQ_INTERNAL" data source "FDP_EA_REQST_FOR_QUOT_INT_SRV".

Using Tcode SEGW, we try to copy the data source "FDP_EA_REQST_FOR_QUOT_INT"  but it doesn't exist.

The standard form is already in place and working fine.

entry.title: HCM Brasil: Como deve ser informada a rubrica de plano de saúde no eSocial S-1.3?
entry.content:
Desde que o governo alterou a Tabela 21 - Códigos de Incidência Tributária da Rubrica para o IRRF do eSocial, incluindo o código “67 - Plano privado coletivo de assistência à saúde” no grupo Dedução do rendimento tributável do IRRF, surgiram diversas interpretações sobre como reportar essa rubrica no evento S-1010 – Tabela de Rubricas.

O entendimento da SAP sempre foi de que a inclusão desse código no leiaute representava um erro, uma vez que os descontos de plano de saúde não podem ser abatidos da base de cálculo do rendimento tributável para fins de retenção mensal do Imposto de Renda.

No dia 18 de dezembro de 2024, foi publicada uma nova informação (item 14.5) na página de FAQ do eSocial ratificando a interpretação da SAP.  

Veja aqui um trecho dessa nova informação disponível:

“...No entanto, o valor descontado pela empresa a título de custeio do plano de saúde não pode ser abatido da base de cálculo do rendimento tributável para fins de retenção do Imposto de Renda na fonte. Assim, esse desconto apenas transita na folha de pagamento do trabalhador, sem impactar a base de cálculo do IRRF. Nesse contexto, o uso de Código de Incidência de IRRF com valor "9" ou "67" não altera o valor final da retenção....”

Até então, a solução da SAP gerava as rubricas relacionadas à plano de saúde com código de incidência de IRRF da rubrica como “79 - Outras isenções”.

Embora essa configuração não impactasse o valor final da retenção do IRRF mensal, ajustamos nossa solução para que as rubricas de desconto de plano de saúde sejam geradas com o código de incidência de IRRF como “09 - Verba transitada pela folha de pagamento de natureza diversa de rendimento ou retenção/isenção/dedução de IR”. A SAP Note 3552889 - eSocial - S-1010 - codIncIRRF for medical assistance wage-types já está disponível para implementar essa adequação.

Além disso, a solução também permite a geração das rubricas de desconto de plano de saúde com código de incidência de IRRF da rubrica igual a “67 - Plano privado coletivo de assistência à saúde”, caso o cliente opte por essa configuração. Para tanto, é necessário seguir as instruções da SAP Note 3532823 - eSocial - S-1010 - New health plan option to report IRRF code.

Em resumo:

  • Anteriormente: As rubricas de desconto de plano de saúde eram geradas com o código de incidência de IRRF igual a “79”.
  • Agora: A partir da SAP Note 3552889 - eSocial - S-1010 - codIncIRRF for medical assistance wage-types, o sistema passa a gerar as rubricas de desconto de plano de saúde com código de incidência de IRRF da rubrica igual a “9”.
  • Opção adicional: Para gerar as rubricas de desconto de plano de código de incidência de IRRF da rubrica igual a “67”, o cliente deve configurar a rubrica conforme a SAP Note 3532823 - eSocial - S-1010 - New health plan option to report IRRF code.

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas.

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias.

Um abraço,        

Leandro D’Avila

entry.title: Down Payment Request for Italy allows Gross Price only
entry.content:
Dear SAP support,

please open Workcenter "Sales Orders" --> "Sales Orders" and search for sales order ***.

Please carry out the following steps for the sales order:

1. Marking the sales order line and clicking on "Create Down payment"
2. Entering "External Reference = Test", "Amount = 10EUR", "Tax Country/Region = Italy"

When entering "Italy" as country, the checkbox for "Gross / Net indicator" is automatically set. We've observed this behaviour only for Italy. When entering a different county, the checkbox isn't set.

We don't want to invoice gross and therefore do not want the checkbox to be ticked. Unfortunately, we cannot uncheck the box manually. Please let us know where this can be changed and where it's configured that the system behaves like this for Italy.

You can also take a look at sales order ***. In the follow-up process we have problems due to the tax code *** and receive inconsistent invoice requests (see e.g. invoice request **). The checkbox shouldn't be checked here either.

As the down payments in the production system must be created urgently with tax code ***, which is not possible at the moment, we need a solution as soon as possible.

Best regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Down Payment Request for Italy allows Gross Price only
entry.content:
Dear customer,

As per our analysis, this is the expected system behavior.

Due to legal requirements, Business ByDesign's system only allows you to release Down Payment Requests in Gross Price for Italy.

This behavior occurs since the System Release 1902 and is documented on KBA 2760906 - Down Payment Requests only with Gross Price [Italy Only] (https://me.sap.com/notes/2760906).

Best Regards,

Marcelo
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Global User ID in SAP Build Work Zone
entry.content:
The Global User ID (userUUID) plays a important role in integration scenarios, acting as a consistent user identifier across system landscapes. This is especially important for scenarios like API-based notification services, where a common identifier ensures seamless data flow and user correlation.

There are two approaches for implementing the Global User ID, but we recommend the first of the two:

Approach 1:

Use SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication and Identity Provisioning to generate and distribute Global User ID. In this case, the attribute is automatically generated by Identity Authentication at user creation. Its value is populated in the Global User ID field for every newly created, imported or provisioned user. After that, Identity Provisioning distributes it to various SAP applications.

Approach 2:

Use your own value for Global User ID and distribute it to SAP applications with SAP Cloud Identity Services. In this case the Identity Authentication is used as a user store, it receives the value in the Global User ID field for every newly created, imported or provisioned user. After that, Identity Provisioning distributes it to various SAP applications. Because the Global User ID field cannot be empty, if you do not provide a value for Global User ID, Identity Authentication generates one for your users. When distributing your own value without SAP Cloud Identity Services, you use your existing identity management processes to provision it to SAP applications (see  the dotted line in the diagram below).

 

Prerequisites and usage

  • Users must have Identity Authentication and Identity Provisioning configured in their landscape.
  • The Global User ID identifier is immutable and unique across technology layers such as user interfaces, APIs, and security tokens, as well as across products and lines of business.
  • It is recommended to use the Global User ID of SAP Cloud Identity Services - Identity Authentication as the federation identifier.
  • Identity Authentication hosts the Global User ID while Identity Provisioning provisions the Global User ID to other systems in the user’s landscape.

 

How to leverage Global ID in SAP Build Work Zone

Provisioning the Global User ID

First, the Global ID is provisioned to SAP Build Work Zone via SCIM API, and consequently transferred to SAP Build Work Zone, as to any other SAP solution, via Identity Provisioning. This applies to both:

  • Role-mapping for content providers within SAP Build Work Zone, all editions as an alternative to role collection-based mapping. See here for more details.
  • Digital Workplace Service (DWS) persistence layer specific to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. This process is part of the Work Zone onboarding. More details about this can be found here.

The Global User ID is transferred (in both scenarios) via the target system transformation / mapping in Identity Provisioning. Using the provided (default) transformation from the SAP Build Work Zone help guide will ensure the attribute is provisioned as required. You will need to verify that the Identity Provisioning source system (for example Cloud Identity) has the Global User ID available.

For example, SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition target system:

 

Trust Setup and Subject Name Identifier

It is recommended to use the Global User ID as the subject name identifier for the trust setup - for both the BTP Subaccount level and direct trust with SAP Build Work Zone. If you want to learn more about the direct OpenID Connect-based trust setup, please see the following blog.

Depending on the configuration of the ‘JAM’ destination, specifically the userIdSource property, it will also require the Global User ID in additional fields – specifically the SCIM.userName. To learn more about the authentication flow and corresponding field usage, please see here.

 

Verify Global User ID information in SAP Build Work Zone (advanced)

To verify the Global ID is properly created in the SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition / SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone, you can check the users using and directly modifying when needed, the SCIM API. This is the same API also utilized by Identity Provisioning (Work Zone target system). When using the API, you can find the Global User ID stored in a dedicated field in the SCIM user record:

For more details related to the SCIM API of advanced edition, how to connect to it, etc. please see the dedicated section in the help guide.

Finally, you can run a report in SAP Build Work Zone administration console by selecting the Company User Detail report (for both internal and external users). This will provide you with the list of all users provisioned to the system and the user values for SCIM.userName, SCIM.id (and others) which can be used as filters / parameters in the SCIM API. Note that in the report the User ID refers to the SCIM.userName and the Uuid refers to SCIM.id.

 

Use Cases

Global User ID plays an important role in SAP Build Work Zone, especially in integration scenarios. For instance, in SAP Task Center, the Global User ID functions as a correlation attribute, linking user identities across different systems. This ensures that tasks from multiple systems can be reliably associated with the same user, enabling a unified task management experience. The integration with the Central Notification Service, which allows Work Zone users to access notifications from various systems, also requires the Global User ID mapping.

Details on this integration setup can be found here (for Task Center) & here (for Notifications).

Another worth mentioning use case is Guided Experiences, where the Global User ID is needed to communicate between SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA), and SAP Build Work Zone. This is instrumental for the UI Wizard in SAP Build Work Zone to retrieve and utilise processes created in SBPA, streamlining workflows and ensuring smooth integration across systems.

Lastly, when creating UI integration cards, the Global User ID is also provided / exposed as user context and can be referenced by the developer of the UI Cards. More details on this built-in card context is available in the SAP-samples GitHub repository:

 

For more info please check:

Help Guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-advanced-edition/sap-build-work-zone-advanced-edition/run-configurator

Learning Journey & Certification: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implement-and-administer-sap-build-work-zone/explaining-the-authentication-flow-of-sap-build-work-zone

entry.title: Re: Code List based on CDS View doesn't show View Entitie...
entry.content: There the following note is given: If data is requested, the method set_data must be called. If the total number of records is requested, the method set_total_number_of_records must be called. Otherwise there will be an error during runtime. The io_response->set_data( ) method is only allowed to fill elements listed by io_request->get_requested_elements( ). System may react with a failure or a short dump if these rules aren't followed. In UI scenarios with Fiori Elements the total number of records is always requested by the UI.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Parameter Asking for Login Information
entry.content:
No, not by default. CR will never retain passwords, huge security breach if anyone can access the data source, they could modify the report and access data they should not be able to see....

Talk to your ERP Administrator, they may be able to set the password for you depending on how they have implemented the reports. It is possible to set the log in info so it doesn't prompt but that all depends on how they have implemented the report security.

You can set the log on info in the report to use Trusted Authentication which should use the initial log on info when the report is saved. Refreshing with a new value should then use the existing DB connection info.

PS - If you are using Business One ERP system add the Business One Tag.

entry.title: Service with Advanced Execution: Process Flow 4 – Service Quotation with Catalogue Price
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution is a new service business process in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This is based on the integration of Service with Maintenance Management.

The concept of Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution is introduced in the following blog:

Execution Status in Service with Advanced Execution

Within Service with Advanced Execution, there are various process flows depending on the business scenario. These process flows are distinguished based on two aspects:

  • How the process starts, and which is the first transaction.
  • What is the trigger for the creation of the maintenance order and eventually the start of planning.

Process Flow 4:

This blog showcases the process flow wherein the initial transaction is the service order quotation, and the maintenance order is not created in the service order quotation phase.

For example, let us assume that a car dealership offers maintenance and repair services for various car models. A customer requests a standard service, such as an engine oil change or brake replacement, and asks for a quotation. The Service Manager has agreed with the customer to perform fixed price billing. The dealership maintains a price master that lists predefined prices for services and parts.

In such scenarios, the service manager will create a quotation to provide a cost estimate for the service requested by the customer. Since the cost of service is agreed upon with the help of existing price master, there is no need of a detailed planning through maintenance order.

Creation of Service Order Quotation

A service order quotation can be created using the ‘Create Service Quotation’ App.

The following details could be filled while creating a service order quotation document:

  • Sold-to-Party
  • Organizational data such as Sales Organization and optionally Service Organization
  • Parties involved such as Service Employee Group and Employee Responsible
  • Execution Order Item with Planning Plant, Work Center, DIP Profile and Billing Relevance
  • Reference Objects

After entering all the details, save the service order quotation document. The service order quotation gets created with an execution order item with lifecycle status as 'Open'.

Status lifecycle in Service with Advanced Execution

                                                           Current state of an execution order item

The state diagram illustrates the status lifecycle of one execution order item along with its corresponding maintenance order (if any) in this process flow. Each block below represents a state displaying 3 different status values. The lifecycle and execution status of an execution order item along with the status of the corresponding maintenance order.

 

Send to Customer

Once the cost estimate is derived for the requested service, either based on the price master or based on any manual intervention, the quotation can be sent to the customer for approval. When the service order quotation is sent to customer, the execution order item status is updated to 'Released'.

Accept and creation of Follow-up Service Order

When the customer accepts the service order quotation, its lifecycle is updated to 'Completed'. A follow-up service order is automatically created, carrying over the existing execution order item.

Further processing of the service order follows the regular process flow.

Summary

The distinct feature in this process flow is the cost estimation without a maintenance order in a service order quotation phase.

 

Further references

To know more about the execution order item and its attributes, please refer following blog:

Deep Dive: Execution Order Item and Billable Maintenance Order

entry.title: Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
entry.content:
In this blog, we will explore how SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management can process electronic invoices received through the Peppol network.

As of January 2025, a new legal mandate in Germany requires businesses to be able to receive invoices electronically from their suppliers. For more information about the law in general, check out this blog New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know.

Multiple Solutions Available

While this blog focuses on handling electronic invoices through the Peppol network, it's important to note that there are other solutions available. SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management also supports electronic invoices received by email. There are additional SAP offerings for handling electronic invoices received from your supplier. To get a complete list, check out the Supported Business Scenarios for Germany.

For more information on some of these alternatives, please refer to the following blogs: 

  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices through the Peppol network in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP

Overview of the Process

The process of receiving  invoices through the Peppol network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management involves several key steps:

  1. Receiving the Invoice
    Your supplier sends an invoice through the Peppol network and the invoice is received at your receiver access point in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.
  2. Validating and Mapping the XML
    SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition validates the electronic invoice and maps it to the standard format used in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.
  3. Verifying and Posting Invoice
    If manual adjustments are needed, such as correcting missing or incorrect fields, you can edit the invoice using the Manage Supplier Invoices Centrally app    . Then you post the invoice.
  4. Further Processing
    An invoice for the received electronic document is created in your connected business system and you can further process it.

For more details about the process, see Receive Invoices from Peppol Network.

How to Get Started?

To establish the communication between the two services, you need to do some configurations as follows:

  • In SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
    Create an instance of the SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management service with the inbound-drc service plan and generate a service key for this instance. See Service Keys.
  • In SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
  1. Set up the connection from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. This connection facilitates the Peppol Exchange process. See Setting Up Connectivity.
  2. Specify client credentials required for your system to communicate with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition using OAuth 2.0 client authentication. See Creating Service Bindings Using OAuth 2.0 Client Authentication for Invoices Received Through the Peppol Network.

    Note: If you are interested in processing electronic invoices received by email, refer to Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

By following these steps, you can ensure compliance with Germany's new electronic invoicing law and streamline your invoicing processes using SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

entry.title: Cutover Business Activities Highlights for a SuccessFactors EC and ONB Implementation
entry.content:
This blog covers the implementation partners to go over the high level business cutover activities for XYZ Company for Employee Central and ONB2.0 , JPB Implementation with Refactoring of RCM, LMS.

Here I have taken the example of the implementation partner as ABC Inc.

Following picture depicts the high-level graphical cutover plan for the XYZ company.

In this picture the freeze is divided three- Master Data Freeze, Overall Freeze and Soft Freeze followed by technical go-live and go-live.

The duration is as follows. These durations can be shorter depending upon the project.

Master data freeze – 2 weeks

Overall Hard Freeze – 1 week

Soft Freeze – 1 week

Technical go live – 1 week

Go live – 1 week

Overview of the terminologies are explained as below ---

 

Master Data Freeze –

  • Only project team members can go and perform RCM. Provide access to all users who are assigned on open requisitions to perform RCM Activities.
  • Only project team members can go and perform ONB 1.0.
  • LMS Internal and External learners can continue learning.
  • ABC project team will do the Cutover as per the plan.
  • Critical / Non-Critical Activities related to Master data for XYZ to be recorded outside SuccessFactors.
  • Only critical transactions will happen in the  legacy systems. Non- critical transactions will be stopped in Legacy Systems as well.
  • Communication should be sent before the Cutover start period.

 

Overall Hard Freeze

  • XYZ  teams cannot get into the SF system
  • ABC project team will do the Cutover as per the plan.
  • Critical / Non-Critical Activities related to all modules for XYZ to be recorded outside SuccessFactors.
  • Only critical transactions will happen in the  legacy systems. Non- critical transactions will be stopped in Legacy Systems as well.
  • Communication should be sent before the Cutover start period.
  • No RCM and ONB1.0 Activity
  • Internal Learners will not have access to do learnings.

 

Soft Freeze

  • Only Project team members from XYZ can enter and do the Data catch up.
  • This will include all critical and non-critical transactions for Employee Master Data as given in Slide 10.
  • RCM Activities as per slide 10
  • LMS Assignment profile adjustments as per slide 10

Technical Go-Live

 

  • Technical Go live will provide HR users to enter data along with XYZ project Team members after technical go live 
  • HR Business can do all the transactions in SuccessFactors now.
  • ESS/MSS changes might need to be done through HR Business Team

 

Go –Live

  • ESS MSS Enabled and End users can now view and update their details.
  •  Legacy system no longer maintained

Now lets understand the XYZ GO LIVE Critical Items and how to handle them during Cutover-

Activities during Freeze period

Legacy System

SuccessFactors System

Hires

•        During Hard Freeze (April 14, 2025 – April 18, 2025) continue to hire until GOLIVE in Legacy system.

•        Employee id generation will happen in Legacy system.

•        During the soft freeze (April 21, 2025 – May 02, 2025)  data catchup will start in SF  by using Migration templates.

•        Reset Employee Id Sequence  in SF after Soft freeze

•        Keep gap for few employee  numbers  while resetting the Employee Id sequence.

Terminations

During Hard Freeze (April 14, 2025 – April 18, 2025) continue Terminations until GOLIVE in Legacy system

During the soft freeze (April 21, 2025 – May 02, 2025) data catchup will start in SF  by using Migration templates (if high in number) or Manual by inserting a term record and removing future conversion record.

Step progressions

During Hard Freeze (April 14, 2025 – April 18, 2025) continue  Step Progressions until GOLIVE in Legacy system.

During the soft freeze (April 21, 2025 – May 02, 2025) data catchup will start in SF. If the dates are after GO LIVE insert a record directly in SF else change the conversion record using Data Migration Templates

Pay changes

During Hard Freeze (April 14, 2025 – April 18, 2025) continue Pay changes  until GOLIVE in Legacy system

During the soft freeze (April 21, 2025 – May 02, 2025) data catchup will start in SF. If the dates are after GO LIVE insert a record directly in SF else change the conversion record using Data Migration Templates

Global Assignment

Concurrent Assignment

Contingent workers

During Hard Freeze (March 31, 2025 – April 18, 2025) continue  GA, CA and CW until GOLIVE in Legacy system

During the soft freeze (April 21, 2025 – May 02, 2025) data catchup will start in SF. If the dates are after GO LIVE insert a record directly in SF else change the conversion record using Data Migration Templates

Position creation

During Hard Freeze (March 31, 2025 – April 18, 2025) continue Position creation in Legacy during Hard Freeze.

During the soft freeze (April 21, 2025 – May 02, 2025) data catchup will start in SF. If the dates are after GO LIVE insert a record directly in SF else change the conversion record using Data Migration Templates.

Job requisition's

Completely stop during hard freeze (March 31, 2025 – April 18, 2025)

During Soft freeze (April 21, 2025 – May 02, 2025) close all requisitions from old template and forward applicants to new template.

Trainings

Complete stop during Hard Freeze (March 31, 2025 – April 18, 2025)

During Soft Freeze April 21, 2025 – May 02, 2025) Assignment profiles should be adjusted. Trainings will continue after Go-live

Now lets see how to handle XYZ GOLIVE NON Critical Transactions during cutover.

Non- Critical Activities during freeze period

Legacy System

SuccessFactors System

Non - Critical Transactions

•        Non-Critical Transactions will be stopped in Legacy systems during Hard Freeze.

•        During Soft Freeze noncritical  transactions  will be performed in Legacy system first before moving to SF.

•        All Non-Critical transactions should be completed before the Hard Freeze and communication should be sent to complete these transactions before hard freeze.

•        During Soft Freeze non-critical  transactions  will be imported via migration templates

•        Any transactions prior to FTSD date and freeze period should be performed in legacy system and then bring to SF Using data migration templates.

•        Any Exceptions of Non-Critical Job and Compensation transactions changes before the FTSD date might require a reload of Data conversion record on Effective dates entities such as Job and comp information OR Purge the employee and reloading him

entry.title: How to generate keys and decrypt audit reports from Ariba Intelligent Configuration Manager
entry.content:
More than once you need download the Ariba audit logs to make more analysis but you find that is not going to be easy because there is a key requested.

You find the help sap site explaning about it but it's no easy make all the described steps, here I will explain with details and step by step this process.

The steps to obtain the audit reports are the following:

  1. Download and install the Git program, only we need the Git Bash app but I recommend you install all by default (SAP recommends use Cygwin but Git works also). This program is neccesary to create the keys and decrypt the logs we need to export.

     

  2. Open the Git Bash program to create your private key and input the following command: openssl genrsa -out myPrivateKey.pem 2048.

     

  3.  Now you need to create your public key typing this command: 
    openssl rsa -in myPrivateKey.pem -pubout -out myPublicKey.pem.
  4. Open a file explorer window and find the folder in the path C:\Users\<<name of your user>> to check the 2 pem files were created.

    or type ls -l *.pem in the Git Bash console to check the existance of the files 

     

  5.  Login to Ariba and access to Intelligent Configuration Manager to generate the audit report. Depending the solution you have the access is diferent this site explains how to access and generate in detail.
  6. Using your favorite text editor, open the myPublicKey.pem file and copy all the content.
  7. Paste the content in the Public key box, make sure there are no empty spaces at the beginning and end of the Pulbic key box.

     

  8. Click in Generate report buttom, if all was made right the report is being generated until complete status and with download link in action colum, otherwise review the previous steps and generate new public and private keys if is necessary.

     

  9. Click in download link, a new window appears and click in download icon now the report zip encrypted is in your download folder.

     

  10. This zip file must have 3 files contained in it:
    • Key
    • IV
    • aes file
  11. Move this zip file to your user folder C:\Users\<<name of your user>> (were your pem files were created).
  12. Now you need decrypt the aes file using your private key file, IV file and Key file. For this you can use the code sample here from Help SAP site and continue to the step 16 or you can use my method that I will explain in the next step.
  13. Unzip the zip file in the same folder C:\Users\<<name of your user>> and don't delete the zip file.
  14. Using your favorite text editor paste this bash code:
    #!/bin/bash
    # Parameters
    PRIVATEKEY="myPrivateKey.pem"
    ZIPFILE="zip file you downloaded from Ariba Intelligent Configuration Manager"
    ENCSUFFIX=".aes"
    echo $ZIPFILE
    # Decrypt the Key and IV
    IV=$(openssl pkeyutl -decrypt -inkey myPrivateKey.pem -in IV)
    KEY=$(openssl pkeyutl -decrypt -inkey myPrivateKey.pem -in Key)
    # Look in the ZIP for files ending in .aes
    # This is the convention used to identify encrypted files
    FILELIST=$(unzip -Z1 "$ZIPFILE" | grep $ENCSUFFIX)
    #echo $FILELIST
    echo $KEY
    echo $IV
    # Decrypt the files that were found
    # Remove the .aes extension from the saved file
    for ENCFILE in $FILELIST
    do
    DECFILE=${ENCFILE%$ENCSUFFIX}
    echo $DECFILE
    echo $ENCFILE
    echo "Decrypting File: $ENCFILE --> $DECFILE"
    openssl enc -aes-256-cbc -d -K $KEY -iv $IV -in $FILELIST -out "$DECFILE"
    done
  15. Only modify the ZIPFILE parameter and add your zip file name like this.

     

  16. Save the file as decrypt.sh in your user folder C:\Users\<<name of your user>>, or use any name you want but make sure save it with .sh extension.
  17. Open again the Git Bash console and run the script: ./decrypt.sh 

     

  18. If everything was right you notice the existence of new zip file with the same name as aes file.
  19. Open or unzip this zip file and finally you will be able to open the excel audit report. 

Some considerations you may take are:

  • It's no neccesary create the pem files everytime you need generate the report.
  • Delete or overwrite the IV and Key file when you need decrypt the a new report.

And that's it, I hope it help you. 😉

This Article is complementary to SAP Help articles; Generating Audit Reports and Decrypting Audit Reports.

entry.title: Customer Onboarding for SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Kickstart Your SAP Digital Manufacturing Journey with Confidence!

Starting a new project can feel overwhelming, but with the right guidance, you’ll hit the ground running. Our Customer Onboarding Journey is designed just for you—an SAP Digital Manufacturing newcomer—so you can move forward with clarity, purpose, and ease.

Where to Begin?

  1. Visit the SAP Digital Manufacturing Onboarding Resource Center

Think of it as your personal roadmap. Inside you will find:

  • How-To Guides: learn how to use SAP for Me step-by-step
  • Clear instructions: how to get started with your system
  • Project Insights: know what to expect and how to plan for your implementation
  1. Attend Our Onboarding Webcasts

Join our quarterly live sessions to make your onboarding journey effortless. You’ll gain access to expert tips, get your questions answered on the spot, and quickly feel confident using SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Can’t join us live? No worries—simply watch the on-demand recordings anytime you like.

Your Onboarding Journey: Four Phases to Success

Everything we share—from the videos and articles in the Onboarding Resource Center to our interactive Webcasts—is built around your onboarding timeline. At every step, you’ll know exactly what to do next and how to stay on track.

Ready to maximize the value of your SAP Digital Manufacturing project?

Explore these resources now and pave the way for lasting success:

 

This is the content in more detail:

Phase 1. Get Started with SAP 

Objective: Ensure you understand the SAP Digital Manufacturing provisioning process, get comfortable with important SAP tools and channel, enable smooth access to your system and how to request support when needed.

Key Highlights:

  • Get a clear picture of your onboarding journey
  • Explore SAP channels like SAP for Me and learn how to set up your s-user account
  • Access your system without hassle
  • Discover how to use SAP Support for both technical and non-technical questions

  

Phase 2. Plan your Implementation

Objective: Learn how to plan, run, and speed up your SAP Digital Manufacturing project using the tools included in your subscription.

Key Highlights:

  • Implement your project with the SAP Activate methodology
  • Keep track of your tasks efficiently with SAP Cloud ALM
  • Understand the importance of assigning clear roles and testing your system thoroughly

 

Phase 3. Get Enabled

 Objective: Get free access to learning resources and tools that help you fully understand and excel with your SAP Digital Manufacturing solution.

 Key Highlights:

  • Start with the SAP Digital Manufacturing Solution Overview
  • Find in-depth guides in the SAP Help Portal
  • Connect with fellow users and experts in the SAP Community
  • Boost your skills through SAP Enterprise Support learning opportunities

 

Phase 4. Prepare to Go Live

Objective: Make sure you have a smooth Go-Live experience and set the stage for long-term success.

Key Highlights:

  • Finish your final tasks confidently
  • Use SAP’s specialized support and services for Go-Live assistance
  • Access ongoing resources to keep improving after launch
  • Stay ahead by embracing new innovations once you’re live

 

In Conclusion

The SAP Digital Manufacturing onboarding journey is designed to guide you every step of the way—from signing your contract to a successful Go-Live. Make the most of each phase to learn, plan, and connect with all the resources available, ensuring long-term growth and achievement.

Your SAP Digital Manufacturing success story starts here—let’s shape the future together!

#FirstExperiencesLast
#SAPDigitalManufacturing
